description_long: This is a bronze or dark metallic helmet reminiscent of ancient Greek or Roman designs, featuring a full head covering with a pronounced nose guard and open eye slits. The surface is mottled with a weathered, aged patina, giving it an antique appearance. Decorative etchings or engravings are visible along the lower edge, adding a touch of craftsmanship and historical authenticity. The helmet is smooth and rounded at the top, tapering down to cover the sides and back of the head completely. There are no visible ventilation holes, visors, or modern retention systems, suggesting a purely historical or ceremonial function. The interior is likely hollow, designed to fit snugly over the wearer's head.
description: A weathered, bronze-colored ancient-style helmet with a full head covering, nose guard, and decorative engravings along the lower edge. The surface is smooth and aged, with no visible modern features.
description_view_0: Front view shows the helmet's open eye slits, pronounced nose guard, and decorative lower edge.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the helmet's smooth, rounded contour and the engraved pattern along the bottom edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the helmet's elongated, rounded back and the continuous decorative band at the base.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the helmet's curvature and engraved lower edge.
